title: Verdict Fold Card
prompt:
Create a production-ready Verdict Fold Card using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original decision card presents a short recommendation and a fold-out rationale. The reader activates Show reasoning to reveal two compact evidence lines beneath a solid divider; selecting Hide reasoning returns the card to its summary. The summary must remain readable in both states, and the visible text must identify whether reasoning is open without relying only on the mint accent. Use a light Bento system with a #F4F5F6 stage, white card, #ECECEE hairline border, 22px corners, soft neutral shadow, #111111 text, and #4A4A4F secondary copy. Mint is the only bright accent. Do not use gradients on the card or any nested surface.

Use semantic article content, a native 44px button, aria-expanded, aria-controls, and a polite live status. Native button behavior must support click, Enter, and Space. Reveal the reasoning using height and opacity with a spring at stiffness 300 and damping 28; rotate the fold mark over 180ms ease-out. Use deterministic local state only: no timers, random values, storage, remote data, network requests, or browser-global reads. Prefer transforms and opacity where possible.

At 320px, stack the evidence lines and keep the control label intact; at detail size, retain a comfortably readable maximum width. Provide a visible 3px focus ring, logical keyboard order, AA contrast, and explicit state wording. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by opening immediately with only a 120ms opacity fade. Return complete client code, pure CSS approximation, and suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML:
...
*/ .verdict-fold{max-width:340px;padding:20px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:22px;background:#fff}.verdict-fold button{min-height:44px;border:1px solid #ececee;border-radius:12px;background:#fff;font-weight:800}.verdict-fold .reasoning{display:none;margin-top:14px;padding-top:12px;border-top:1px solid #ececee}.verdict-fold button[aria-expanded=true]+.reasoning{display:block}.verdict-fold button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.verdict-fold *{transition:none}}

demoCode:
